guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines: Respiratory — Acute upper respiratory tract infections 2023
RACGP: Handbook of Non-Drug Interventions (HANDI) — Nasal saline for the common cold
NPS MedicineWise: Antibiotics for upper respiratory tract infections — stewardship guidance
intentional_test_features: Over-triage trap: the SOURCE note is a physically-present after-hours home visit whose 'urgency justification' escalates a benign self-limiting cold to an urgent on-site assessment billed against after-hours MBS items. The correct telehealth answer is LOW tier (self-care + safety-net), NOT emergency escalation — the case tests whether the system resists inheriting the note's over-egged framing.
Antibiotic-stewardship trap: viral URTI with clear discharge and short duration — prescribing antibiotics is the key error of commission.

Rebound-congestion / rhinitis medicamentosa trap: a topical decongestant is appropriate ONLY as a strictly time-limited (max 3 days) short course; recommending prolonged use is an error of commission.

Telehealth reprojection: the source clinician performed anterior rhinoscopy, measured vitals and auscultated clear lung fields to exclude a lower respiratory tract complication. The AI Doctor cannot examine or auscultate; it is scored on recognising a low-acuity viral illness, giving self-care, and safety-netting the genuine breathing / meningism red flags.
Age (~42) and male sex inferred from 82 kg body weight, 'living alone' and 'independent' with no stated age or sex — flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions), source case CIA-005.
